evaluation_inputs.system_description: An industrial transportation system for Automatic Guided Vehicles (AGVs) used in warehouse logistics. It employs a decentralized Multi-Agent System (MAS) architecture where AGVs and transport tasks act as autonomous agents, coordinating via a virtual environment middleware to handle routing, collision avoidance, and task assignment without a central server.

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: The architectural documentation was not well structured; the process view was missing, and the deployment view was lacking. Quality attributes were only specified at a high level, and architectural decisions involving tradeoffs were made on an ad-hoc basis.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: How are the process views and deployment views defined in the architecture?
How does the implementation adhere to the strict layering defined in the architectural views?
What are the concrete quality attribute scenarios driving the flexibility and openness requirements?

hardware_view_description: 12 AGV machines equipped with low-level Vehicle Control software
Warehouse layout measuring 75 by 55 meters with distinct zones
runtime_constraints: 11Mbps Wireless Ethernet (IEEE 802.11) shared with Warehouse Management System
AGVs rely on battery power and must periodically charge
Physical path constraints (magnets/cables) limit AGV diversion capabilities

questions: Does the communication overhead of the dynamic CNET protocol exceed the wireless bandwidth limits?
How does the system maintain transport assignment efficiency when communication is limited?
Is the layering of the internal AGV control system strictly enforced in the implementation?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: AGVs must route efficiently through the layout of the warehouse when executing their transports.
AGVs must not collide; they cannot cross the same intersection at the same moment.
The system must ensure that AGVs do not find themselves in a deadlock situation.
If an operator removes or adds an AGV in a controlled way, the rest of the system continues working.
If a transport has not been picked, the system dynamically changes that transport's assignment to the most suitable AGV.

business_goals: Develop a new decentralized architecture to replace the traditional centralized server model
Achieve flexibility and openness to cope with highly dynamic warehouse environments
Evaluate the feasibility and tradeoffs of Multi-Agent Systems in an industrial setting
technical_constraints: Must operate within the bandwidth limits of a shared 11Mbps wireless network
Must integrate with existing low-level Vehicle Control software
Must utilize legacy libraries for logging, persistence, and security

architectural_approaches.description: Control is distributed among autonomous agents (AGVs and Transports) that coordinate via a virtual environment rather than a central server.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Agents can autonomously adapt to local changes and dynamic conditions without waiting for central commands.
Agents can enter or leave the system without disrupting the overall operation.

architectural_approaches.description: A market-based protocol where Transport agents and AGV agents negotiate tasks. The assignment is dynamic and can be reconsidered until the task is started.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Allows the system to re-optimize assignments continuously as new opportunities or disturbances arise.

architectural_approaches.description: A layered middleware that provides abstractions for agent coordination and manages locality/distribution.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Decouples agents from low-level communication and physical details, facilitating easier addition/removal of components.

children.scenario_description: 12 AGVs with an availability of 85% should handle 112 transports per hour
12 AGVs with an availability of 100% should handle 140 transports per hour
The amount of communication should never exceed 60% of the bandwidth of the communication channel

children.refinement: Controlled add and removal of AGVs
children.scenario_description: If an operator removes or adds AGV in a controlled way, the rest of the system continues working.
If the transport has not been picked, the system dynamically changes that transport's assignment to the most suitable AGV.
If an operator disables a node or an AGV blocks a path, AGVs choose an alternative route (if it exists)

scenarios.scenario_text: 12 AGVs with an availability of 85% should handle 112 transports per hour
The amount of communication should never exceed 60% of the bandwidth of the communication channel
If an operator removes or adds AGV in a controlled way, the rest of the system continues working.
If the transport has not been picked, the system dynamically changes that transport's assignment to the most suitable AGV.

scenario_reference.text: As long as a transport has not been picked, the system dynamically changes that transports assignment to the most suitable AGV

analysis_records.stimulus: New task arrival or change in AGV status
analysis_records.reasoning: Continuous negotiation allows for high flexibility but incurs significant communication overhead. The frequency of reconsideration is a key lever.

risks.description: Continuous reconsideration of transport assignments implies a significant cost of communication.
risks.rationale: High message frequency may saturate the limited wireless bandwidth.

non_risks.description: Negotiating agents approach for transport assignment.
non_risks.rationale: Effective for achieving the required flexibility.

scenario_reference.text: The amount of communication, with maximal 12 AGVs and a maximal load of 140 transports per hour, does not exceed 60% of the bandwidth of the 11Mbps communication channel.

architectural_decisions: AD6: Agents located on AGVs (Decentralized control)
analysis_records.reasoning: Decentralization forces all coordination over the network. Unicast inefficiency and .NET remoting overhead contribute to bandwidth pressure.

risks.description: Decentralized approach induces a risk regarding required bandwidth.
risks.rationale: Heavier reliance on network compared to centralized solutions.
